headings.style: uppercase, tight-tracking (tracking-tighter), font-normal or font-bold depending on scale
headings.usage: Use for all Hero headings, section titles, card titles, stats, and numbers. Gives an imposing, premium, commercial feel.

body.style: clean, modern sans-serif. loose leading for paragraphs (leading-relaxed), tight for UI elements
body.usage: Use for all descriptions, labels, forms, tables, buttons, and navigation.

rules: No pure #000000. Use #0A0A0A for deepest depth.
Gradients should be used sparingly, mostly as subtle background glows (radial-gradient) behind hero or key stats.
Primary CTA buttons must be Gold (#D4AF37) with black (#0A0A0A) text.
Interactive states must always increase contrast (e.g. hover:bg-[#B3922E]).

visual_enhancers.shadows: Use subtle colored glows instead of drop-shadows on dark backgrounds. e.g. shadow-[0_0_20px_rgba(212,175,55,0.15)] for premium cards.
visual_enhancers.borders: 1px solid #2A2A2A. Use border-white/5 for glass effects.
visual_enhancers.surfaces: Glassmorphism for sticky navs and mobile bottom bars: bg-[#0A0A0A]/80 backdrop-blur-xl border-b border-white/10.
visual_enhancers.textures: Add a subtle noise/grain overlay (opacity 0.03) to the main background to prevent it from feeling like 'flat digital void'.

component_rules.buttons: Pill-shaped (rounded-full) or sharp rectangles (rounded-md). Since Bebas Neue is sharp, let's use slightly rounded (rounded-md) to match the industrial/logistics vibe. Gold background for primary, transparent with 1px border for secondary.
component_rules.cards: Subtle borders (border-white/10), dark background (#141414). Groupage cards need to feel like 'tickets' or 'cargo manifests' - use dashed lines or perforated edges as a creative detail.
component_rules.forms: Inputs must have dark bg (bg-[#0A0A0A]), visible border (border-white/20), and glow gold on focus (focus:border-[#D4AF37] focus:ring-1 focus:ring-[#D4AF37]).
component_rules.status_badges: Must use the accent colors. Validated = Green bg with 10% opacity + Green text. Pending = Orange. Alert/Failed = Red. Premium/Completed = Gold.
component_rules.tables: Use for Admin/Member dashboard. Dense, clean lines, hover effects on rows (hover:bg-white/5).
component_rules.timeline: For logistics timeline, use vertical line with glowing dots. Gold dot for current status, green for past, grey for future.

grids_and_layouts.spacing: Generous spacing. p-6 or p-8 inside cards. gap-6 to gap-8 for grids.
grids_and_layouts.landing_page: Bento grids for 'How it works' and 'Savings Comparator'. Asymmetrical layouts to build tension. Large typography.
grids_and_layouts.dashboard: High-density Grid. Strict hierarchy. Sidebar + Header + Main Content.
grids_and_layouts.mobile_first: Ensure all tables have horizontal scroll on mobile. Bottom navigation bar for mobile app feel. Stacked cards for groupages.

motion.hover: Cards slightly lift (-translate-y-1) and border glows.
motion.page_transitions: Fade and slight slide up (y: 20 -> 0) using framer-motion.
motion.marquee: Use a slow marquee (react-fast-marquee) for displaying active groupages or trusted transitaire logos.
motion.micro_interactions: Icon rotation or scale on button hover.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure all text against dark background passes APCA.
accessibility.focus: Visible focus rings on all interactive elements.
accessibility.aria: Use aria-labels for icon-only buttons (like chat or document viewer).
accessibility.testing: Add data-testid to all buttons, links, inputs, and major sections.

images.description: Modern African businessman using phone, smiling. Great for mobile onboarding or success state.
Local African merchant sitting, authentic feel. Good for target audience representation.
Close up of cargo containers. Use for groupage background or category image.
Large body of water with cranes. Good for background textures or timeline illustration.
Bustling industrial port with cranes. Perfect for main landing page hero background with a dark overlay.
Close-up electronic microchips. Use as thumbnail for an electronics groupage.

3d_libraries.recommendation: Use framer-motion for UI transitions. If visualizing the China->Africa route on the landing page, consider react-simple-maps or a lightweight SVG path animation (tracing beam) rather than full 3D to keep performance high on mobile networks.

instructions_to_main_agent: You are building 'SilkRoute', a B2B group-buying SaaS platform.
Start by setting up the Tailwind config with the specified colors (obsidian, gold, alert, success).
Import Bebas Neue and DM Sans from Google Fonts in the index.html or global CSS.
Build the UI Mobile-First. This is critical as the target audience uses mobile devices heavily.
Use Shadcn components for complex logic (Select, Dialog, Form), but heavily customize their styling (remove white backgrounds, use #141414, change borders to #2A2A2A).
Do NOT use generic SaaS blue anywhere. The primary interactive color is Gold (#D4AF37).
Ensure the Savings Comparator is a highly visual, interactive card showing 'Local vs SilkRoute' price.
For the Groupage Cards, make them feel like logistics manifests. Include a progress bar for 'volume filled' and a countdown timer.
The App must have a language toggle (FR/EN) in the header or sidebar.
Use Sonner for toast notifications (alerts, success messages).
universal_guidelines: Never center-align paragraphs. Left align for readability.
Do not use transparent backgrounds for dropdowns, navs, or modals. Always use a solid dark color or heavy blur with high opacity.
All interactive elements MUST include a data-testid attribute.
Use 2-3x more padding than standard. If you think p-4 is enough, use p-6 or p-8.
Avoid AI slop: no generic gradients, no overused emojis in UI, no floating blobs. Keep it industrial, sharp, premium.
Components MUST use named exports, Pages MUST use default exports.
Avoid applying 'transition: all' to prevent breaking transforms. Be specific (e.g., transition-colors, transition-transform).
